title: heliotropium marifolium subsp. wallichii
intro: Heliotropium marifolium subsp. wallichii, commonly known as the Indian heliotrope, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family, Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Heliotropium marifolium subsp. wallichii include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
